blockedAssertions.blockedBy: Code and tests confirm branching dialogue trees with multiple selectable choices, prerequisite-gated options, long-dialogue scrolling, and save/load support for dialogue state. Different branches can trigger different quest/reward outcomes. However, no browser/user-surface run was captured here, so screenshot-backed UI confirmation is still missing.

Source and tests show end-to-end escort wiring: escort quest data, active escort NPC spawn, NPC-follow logic, NPC-only destination completion gate, NPC death failure, quest log labels/progress, and re-acceptance after failure. But this validation is code-backed rather than browser-observed, so the actual user-surface flow remains unconfirmed.

Defend quest wave progression, target HP/failure logic, and investigate clue discovery/progress are wired in code and covered by tests. Quest log shows wave counts and clue counts, and minimap markers are implemented. The contract also requires a visible target HP bar, enemy-wave user flow, and interactable clue objects with visual indicators; those user-surface aspects were not directly exercised and cannot be claimed from code alone.

Craft-and-deliver quests are defined with three ordered phases, quest log phase display, NPC-triggered craft/deliver advancement, and save/load preservation. Tests verify collect→craft→deliver sequencing and gating. This supports the logic, but the actual in-game crafting-at-NPC flow was not exercised through the browser, so full end-to-end UX confirmation is still missing.

New quest types are integrated into quest data/types, Chinese quest-log labels and colored tags, QuestSystem.updateProgress handling, QUEST_COMPLETED emission, rewards on turn-in, minimap objective markers, and NPC quest marker updates. However, without user-surface execution, I cannot fully prove marker transitions or real turn-in presentation from gameplay alone.

Quest save/load preservation for new quest types is strongly supported: SaveData includes quests, ZoneScene serializes/restores quest progress, and tests verify persisted progress for escort/defend/investigate/craft quests. Dialogue state persistence is also present. Quest log pagination exists, but the contract's expanded-count scenario (42+ existing plus new) was not exercised in the UI, so that portion remains unverified.

Browser validation could not reliably drive the Phaser canvas to a fresh new game in this isolated session; code-backed verification confirms all five zone mini-bosses are elite, have 3+ Chinese pre-fight dialogue lines, persist non-repeat tracking via miniBossDialogueSeen, and define guaranteed enhanced loot tables.

Real browser collection flow was blocked by fragile canvas interaction, but code-backed verification confirms each zone has at least 4 lore collectibles with distinct visuals, Chinese lore text well over 50 characters, hidden/non-obvious placement flags, lore popups, and lore log progress strings like "X/Y 收集" in the UI.

Could not run enough exploratory movement through the Phaser canvas to trigger live events, so verification remained code-backed: RandomEventSystem enforces a 30000ms cooldown, blocks in safe zones and combat, defines ambush and treasure cache events with Chinese warnings, and ZoneScene routes those events into gameplay/log output.

Live merchant/rescue/puzzle event execution was blocked by the same Phaser canvas control limitation, but code-backed verification shows wandering merchant, rescue, and environmental puzzle events are implemented with zone-scaled data, Chinese UI/log strings, shop payload wiring, rescue rewards, and puzzle interaction handling.

The browser session reached the game shell but reliable in-canvas travel/interaction to NPCs, hidden areas, and sub-dungeon portals was not achievable. Code-backed verification confirms every zone has 2 new NPCs with Chinese dialogue, 1 hidden area with reward/discovery text, 3 story decorations, and at least 2 zones expose sub-dungeon entrances with enter/exit flow and companion-carrying scene restart data.

deferralNote.message: 11 blocked assertions (VAL-STORY-001 through VAL-STORY-011) were deferred from story-content milestone to visual-polish milestone. They are now assigned to the cross-area-integration feature for browser-based user-surface validation during visual-polish. Their status has been reset to pending in validation-state.json. VAL-STORY-012 (passed) remains in zone-content-expansion.
deferralNote.reason: Browser/Phaser canvas interaction was too fragile for isolated user-surface validation; assertions will be validated during cross-area integration testing
